confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 4 reflects high but not maximum corrigibility: the EU's pluralist architecture and legal correction mechanisms are strong, but democratic-backsliding within member states and the Brexit precedent reveal limits to the order's self-correction capacity. Not maximum because the order cannot fully enforce its own normative standards on member states without triggering exit or legitimacy crises.

reviewNote: Score value 4/5 defensible: pluralist-reordering mechanism (Treaty on European Union's consensus architecture) represents high corrigibility. Confidence 0.60 correctly reflects that democratic backsliding in member states and Brexit's demonstration of exit-mechanism limits are genuine threats to the corrigibility claim. Hold at score-review until chain expansion; flag democratic-backsliding cases as a priority secondary source for the chain.
